productive_behavior: The independent plan audit and deterministic plan lint passed before execution.

After one ordinary Python code repair, Python exec-002 and R exec-003 independently recovered the exact planted mean difference, Welch statistic, confidence interval, and Hedges g while recording both sites A and B.
Successful exec-007 bound the exact language-artifact hashes into reconciliation.json with all_pass and reconciliation_passed true and every checked absolute difference below 1e-6.

Repair 1 acquired the cited PubMed abstract, removed an unsupported observational classification, corrected inline-citation use, and replaced an excessive-precision table with a rectangular 30-row reader table.
The immutable run passed final deterministic validation, Gemma report and multimodal display review, and the repository's known-effect scorer at 18/18.
release_blockers: The effect-interval preflight normalized assigned zero only when x and y were positional arguments. The successful script instead called errorbar(x=[y_pos], y=[md], xerr=...) after y_pos = 0, so the equivalent transposition escaped the execution gate.
The post-execution figure-source validator had the same positional-only assumption and did not resolve the y_pos assignment, so deterministic report validation also admitted the misleading raster.

Panel B contains no plotted estimate or confidence interval in its data region: the script placed x at zero, y at the mean difference of 5, then restricted y to -0.5 through 0.5. The visible green point and interval are only the legend handle.
The registered caption and alt text falsely describe panel B as a forest plot displaying the mean difference and 95% confidence interval.
Gemma's multimodal audit returned pass with visual-clearance attestations, and the mechanical evaluator scored 18/18, so native raster inspection again overruled every automated verdict.

rejected_figure.defect: Panel B shows a null line, annotation, and legend example but no estimate or confidence interval in the plotting region.

implemented_repairs: Execution preflight now extracts Matplotlib x and y coordinates from either positional arguments or x=/y= keywords before applying assigned-constant and interval-dataflow checks.
The post-execution figure-source validator now applies the same positional-or-keyword normalization and resolves exact numeric constants through prior assignments.
Regression tests cover the exact y_pos = 0 plus errorbar(x=[y_pos], y=[mean_diff], xerr=...) syntax while preserving the valid estimate-on-x form.
disposition: Rejected despite terminal supported, final deterministic and independent-review passes, and evaluator 18/18. The numerical analysis, reconciliation, and corrected table are sound, but the registered figure and its caption are materially misleading. Both the pre-execution and post-execution source gates now reject the exact keyword-coordinate transposition.
